CREATE TABLE test_dates (id Int32, date_string String);
SELECT id, parseDateTimeBestEffort(date_string) AS parsed_date FROM test_dates;
SELECT id, date_string, parseDateTimeBestEffort(date_string) AS parsed_date FROM test_dates WHERE parsed_date IS NOT NULL;

WITH now() AS ts_now, (SELECT arrayJoin(['2020-01-01 00:00:00', '2021-02-28 23:59:59'])) AS date_values SELECT date_values, parseDateTimeBestEffort(date_values) AS parsed_date FROM system.numbers LIMIT 2;
WITH now() AS current_time, (SELECT ts_now - INTERVAL 1 DAY AS yesterday) AS date_limits SELECT parseDateTimeBestEffort('2020/12/31 15:00:00') AS parsed_yesterday, yesterday FROM date_limits;
WITH ('10 20:19') AS syslog_input SELECT parseDateTimeBestEffort(syslog_input) AS parsed_syslog_date;
SELECT date_string, parseDateTimeBestEffort(date_string, 'UTC') AS utc_parsed_date FROM test_dates WHERE date_string LIKE '%/%';
CREATE TABLE parsed_dates AS SELECT id, parseDateTimeBestEffort(date_string) AS parsed_date FROM test_dates;
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM parsed_dates WHERE parsed_date IS NOT NULL;
WITH (SELECT parseDateTimeBestEffort('10 20:19')) AS time_result SELECT time_result;
SELECT id, date_string FROM test_dates WHERE parseDateTimeBestEffort(date_string) < now() ORDER BY parsed_date DESC;
